channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Word of the Day: perfidious
item.description: This word has appeared in nine articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Britain’s Likely Next Prime Minister Made His Name in Manchester. Here’s How.
item.description: Andy Burnham was mayor of Greater Manchester for nine years. His time there came to define his identity — and his pitch to lead Britain.

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times

item.title: The World Cup Gave Mexicans a Fantasy They Won’t Soon Forget
item.description: The Mexican team’s early success united and electrified the nation, giving fans a reason to fill the streets in celebration instead of protest.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and James Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mexican fans during the World Cup round of 16 match against England at the Azteca Stadium on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Live Updates: Crowds Gather in Tehran for Ayatollah’s Funeral Procession
item.description: Tens of thousands of Iranians have viewed the casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was exalted by some and despised by others. He was killed four months ago at the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: A News Site Published a Video of a School Lockdown. Then It Was Gone.
item.description: A New Jersey judge ordered New Brunswick Today to remove the video and barred it from writing about the episode, alarming First Amendment advocates.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag
item.category: New Brunswick High School (New Brunswick, NJ)

media:content.media:credit: James Estrin/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Charlie Kratovil, the editor and founder of the New Brunswick Today news site, published video of a high school student’s arrest.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 6, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: FIFA Reverses Suspension of Top U.S. Scorer After Trump Call
item.description: FIFA reversed a suspension against Folarin Balogun, the top scorer on the U.S. men’s soccer team this World Cup, after President Trump called FIFA’s president, Gianni Infantino, to ask for a review.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump received the first FIFA Peace Prize from the head of the soccer governing body, Gianni Infantino, in December.

item.title: China Test Fires Long-Range Ballistic Missile in the Pacific
item.description: China’s firing of a submarine-launched missile came as Australia secured more defense deals with Pacific Island nations. Countries expressed concern about the test.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim and Chris Buckley

media:content.media:credit: Chinese People’s Liberation Army News and Communication Center

item.title: ‘The Five-Star Weekend’ and 6 More Shows to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The adaptation of Elin Hilderbrand’s book airs and the new iteration of “Little House on the Prairie” begins.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Greg Gayne/Peacock
media:content.media:description: From left: Regina Hall, Jennifer Garner, D’Arcy Carden and Chloë Sevigny in “The Five Star Weekend.”

item.title: Andy Burnham, the UK’s Likely Next Prime Minister, Made His Name in Manchester. Here’s How.
item.description: Andy Burnham was mayor of Greater Manchester for nine years. His time there came to define his identity — and his pitch to lead Britain.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle and Andrew Testa

item.description: As mayor, Mr. Burnham brought Manchester’s trams and privatized bus services into a single tightly regulated public system, the Bee Network.

item.title: How Manchester’s Bee Buses and Trams Helped Fuel Andy Burnham’s Rise
item.description: Andy Burnham brought Greater Manchester’s public transit back under public control, making buses more frequent and capping fares.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Bee Network bus at a station in Manchester in June. The worker bee is a longstanding emblem of the city.

item.title: 10-Minute Challenge: The Ceiling at Grand Central
item.description: We’d like you to look at one piece of art for 10 minutes, uninterrupted.
item.dc:creator: Larry Buchanan and Karthik Patanjali

media:content.media:credit: Karthik Patanjali/The New York Times

item.title: No Corrections: July 6, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, July 6, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Wartime Sign for Russians: ‘Out of Gas’
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, July 6, 2026.

item.title: Typhoon Bavi Pounds Guam and Northern Mariana Islands
item.description: There was an island-wide power outage on Saipan, the capital of the Northern Mariana Islands, as the Category 5 storm approached.
item.dc:creator: Josie Moyer and Mar-Vic Cagurangan

media:content.media:credit: Yuichi Yamazaki/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Strong winds brought by Super Typhoon Bavi hit Guam on Monday.

item.title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rock Kyiv on Eve of NATO Summit
item.description: Ballistic missiles were fired into the capital of Ukraine, as Russia launched its second major attack in less than a week. At least seven people were killed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:credit: Efrem Lukatsky/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A building damaged by a Russian missile and drone attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, on Monday.

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3 Episode 3 Recap: The Rat Banquet
item.description: Rhaenyra’s advent to power starts off rough and doesn’t get much better.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:credit: Ollie Upton/HBO
media:content.media:description: It’s not easy being queen: Emma D’Arcy in “House of the Dragon.”

item.title: How Trump Put Himself in the Middle of America’s 250th
item.description: The president placed his image and initiatives front and center, blurring the line, critics said, between loyalty to nation and loyalty to him.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump in Bismarck, N.D., on Wednesday. Historians say he has made an aggressive push to revert to eras he considers to have embodied American greatness.

item.title: First American Woman Rows Solo From California to Hawaii
item.description: Kelsey Pfendler, a Grand Canyon river-rafting guide, completed the journey of more than 2,300 miles in a rowboat named Lily in just under 44 days, according to data from the Ocean Rowing Society International.
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick

media:content.media:credit: Eugene Tanner/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Ala Wai Harbor in Honolulu last July. Late Friday night, Kelsey Pfendler, a Grand Canyon river-rafting guide, pulled into the harbor after rowing solo from California, spending more than 43 days at sea.

item.title: New York Braces for Heavy Rain: Latest Forecast
item.description: Emergency officials warned that flash flooding was possible on Monday as powerful storms were expected to move through the region.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCann

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 6, 2026
item.description: Alex Eaton-Salners proves himself a key figure in the crossword scene.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Robert Stolarik for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: We never tire of adding visual humor to the Wordplay column.

item.title: All Passengers Rescued After Seaplane Crashes in East River
item.description: A seaplane flying from the Hamptons with eight people aboard crashed into the East River near the Manhattan waterfront on Sunday, according to officials. The Fire Department rescued everyone on board.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Police and Fire Department boats pulled up within 90 seconds after a seaplane crash in the East River, a passenger said.

item.title: Israel’s Government Threatens to Ignore a Top Court Ruling
item.description: A resolution by the country’s cabinet escalated a long-running conflict between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and the country’s judiciary. A senior government official later walked that back.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Johnatan Reiss

media:content.media:credit: Ilia Yefimovich/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at a public event on Sunday with his wife, Sara.

item.title: Shootings in Nine States Mar Fourth of July Celebrations
item.description: Shootings occurred across the country, including in New York, Florida, Texas, Illinois and Tennessee. More than 50 people were shot, and at least five people have died.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam and Maia Spoto

media:content.media:credit: Kyle Mazza/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: New York police officers investigate the scene of a shooting early Sunday morning in Brooklyn.

item.title: Can Iran Keep Its Edge?
item.description: Closing the Strait of Hormuz was a huge source of leverage during the war. But playing the same card over and over is risky.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In Tehran, last week.

item.title: Bertrand Grébaut, Influential French Chef, Is Dead at 44
item.description: He ventured to the Right Bank to create Septime, a restaurant focused on vegetable-oriented cuisine “that changed the trajectory of French cooking.”
item.dc:creator: Elaine Sciolino

media:content.media:credit: Emmanuel Dunand/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Bertrand Grébaut in 2012 at a food festival in Brooklyn. He avoided social media, saying “I encourage the public and clients to restart taking pleasure in the moment shared around a table and stop consuming photos.”

item.title: Trump Asked FIFA to Review U.S. Player’s Suspension. Now He’s Eligible to Play.
item.description: The reversal of Folarin Balogun’s World Cup suspension is highly unusual. It was the first time since 1962 that FIFA has nullified a suspension for a red card received during the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Tariq Panja

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump received the first FIFA Peace Prize from the head of the soccer governing body, Gianni Infantino, in December.

item.title: The Main Attraction at Africa’s Most Lucrative Horse Race? Black Wealth.
item.description: In South Africa, where the Black majority was excluded from the economy for generations, the annual Durban July event is a chance to celebrate luxury and high society.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Joao Silva

media:content.media:description: This is the 130th year of Durban July, a daylong horse racing event in Durban, South Africa. Doris Mataboge, in blue, cheered as her horse won.

item.title: La criptomoneda de Trump ha causado pérdidas de millardos de dólares a sus inversores
item.description: Un informe de una empresa de análisis detalla cómo les ha ido a quienes compraron la memecoin de Trump: la mayoría de los inversores minoristas han perdido dinero, mientras que a los más experimentados les ha ido mejor.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton and David Yaffe-Bellany

media:content.media:credit: Elizabeth Frantz para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Manifestantes frente al Trump National Golf Club en Sterling, Virginia, antes de una cena privada para inversores en las criptomonedas del presidente Donald Trump en mayo.

item.title: ‘Minions’ Opens at No. 1 but Falls Far Short of Earlier Films in the Franchise
item.description: The movie generated $36.4 million in ticket sales in North America for the holiday weekend, a 62 percent decline from 2024, the last time a “Minions” movie was in theaters.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling

media:content.media:credit: Illumination/Universal Pictures, via Universal Pictures, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A scene from “Minions & Monsters,” beloved by critics for its ode to old Hollywood.

item.title: Their Seaplane Trip From the Hamptons Ends With a Scary 15 Seconds
item.description: A teenager traveling with her grandmother filmed a video as the seaplane they were flying in crashed in the East River. They were rescued along with four other passengers, the pilot and a crew member.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius and Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Police and Fire Department boats pulled up within 90 seconds after a seaplane crash in the East River, a passenger said.

item.title: Mallory McMorrow Suspends Senate Bid in Democrats’ Heated Michigan Primary
item.description: The state senator sought to carve a middle path between a left-wing candidate and one backed by the party establishment, but she gained little traction.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Jose Juarez/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: State Senator Mallory McMorrow’s polling and fund-raising failed to keep pace with those of her two main rivals, Representative Haley Stevens and Dr. Abdul El-Sayed.

item.title: More Severe Weather Looms as Over 800,000 Remain Without Power
item.description: Strong storms knocked out power this week to customers from Oklahoma to Connecticut. As they waited on Sunday for the lights to come back on, the forecast called for more severe weather.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz and Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:credit: Finn Gomez/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Lightning flashes in the sky during Fourth of July celebrations on Saturday in Washington. More than 800,000 customers across the United States remained without power on Sunday after a series of storms.

item.title: Elle Woods vuelve a las pantallas, esta vez en su adolescencia
item.description: Tras una audición insólita y miles de aspirantes, Lexi Minetree consiguió el papel con una mezcla de disciplina, encanto y una fe absoluta en el personaje.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik and Thea Traff

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: White House Criticizes Smithsonian Museum for ‘Extreme Political Activism’
item.description: A scathing report released on the Fourth of July says the National Museum of American History downplays the role of the founders while emphasizing social justice.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley, Robin Pogrebin and Jennifer Schuessler

media:content.media:credit: Annabelle Gordon/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A report by the White House’s Domestic Policy Council said, “To the extent that there is a story told at the Smithsonian’s National Museum of American History, it is not one of ‘the victory of freedom and genius of our country’ but one of regret, tragedy and shame.”

item.title: Sparks Flew, But Star Sightings Were Scarce in the Hamptons after Taylor Swift’s New York Wedding
item.description: Restaurant owners, residents and visitors alike were surprised by the relative lack of notable faces in the Hamptons this Fourth of July weekend.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Manners and Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Emma Rose Milligan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Zach Erdem, owner of 75 Main, holds a sparkler-topped sheet cake during a Taylor Swift wedding-themed event on Friday evening at the restaurant and lounge in Southampton.

item.title: Navy Ends Search for Missing Crew Member After Arabian Sea Helicopter Crash
item.description: The cause of the crash was still under investigation, but the military said it was not the result of hostile fire. A total of 14 U.S. service members have been killed in the war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jon Gambrell/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The military has suspended the search for a crashed MH-60 Seahawk helicopter in the Persian Gulf like the one pictured here, in a file photo from 2018.

item.title: Taylor Swift Wedding: What Her Lyrics Say About Marriage
item.description: While many details of her wedding remain unknown, the songs from her 20-year-career tell us plenty about her thoughts on the institution.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The scene outside Madison Square Garden announcing the marriage of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce.

item.title: Klaus Mäkelä Plants His Flag in Opera With Strauss’s ‘Frau’
item.description: “Die Frau Ohne Schatten” is a difficult opera for any conductor. Klaus Mäkelä chose it for his first new production, in front of a global audience.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone

item.title: At Site Where Iran’s Slain Leader Lies, Some See a Symbol of His Failures
item.description: The complex where mourners are paying their respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei this weekend is unfinished after nearly 40 years. Critics say it is emblematic of the unfulfilled promises of his rule.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners at the funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the Grand Mosalla complex in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Madonna se ha convertido en un símbolo de nuestro miedo a envejecer
item.description: Si ella no puede resistirse a las fuerzas que exigen que las mujeres sigan aparentando juventud, ¿qué posibilidades tenemos las demás?
item.dc:creator: Glynnis MacNicol

item.title: Exhausted by Iran War, Tehran Transforms for Khamenei’s Funeral
item.description: Throughout the capital, the government is using Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral to project strength at a time of great uncertainty for Iran.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Emile Ducke

media:content.media:description: People in central Tehran on Saturday, below an image of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as a younger man. Portraits of the slain leader can be seen across the capital.

item.title: Mike Wallace, Who Wrote an Acclaimed History of New York, Dies at 83
item.description: His 1998 book, “Gotham,” which told the city’s story to 1898, focused on social and economic conflict. It won a Pulitzer Prize and inspired two sequels.
item.dc:creator: Sam Roberts
item.category: Greater Gotham: A History of New York City from 1898 to 1919 (Book)
Gotham: A History of New York City to 1898 (Book)
Gotham at War: A History of New York City from 1933 to 1945 (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Elizabeth D. Herman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mike Wallace in 2017. The Pulitzer committee said that “Gotham,” which he wrote with Edwin G. Burrows, tied many strands “into a single narrative tapestry that reads like a fast-paced novel.”

item.title: This Olive Oil Zucchini Bread Is Just Sweet Enough
item.description: Cinnamon, nutmeg and lemon zest give it an inimitable perfume.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Yossy Arefi.
media:content.media:description: Melissa Clark’s olive oil zucchini bread.

item.title: What Does It Mean to Love America?
item.description: America celebrates its 250th birthday this year. The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein asks the civil rights lawyer Bryan Stevenson what he thinks American greatness is really about.

item.title: Bill Archer, Influential Texas Congressman, Is Dead at 98
item.description: Serving for 30 years, he eventually wrote virtually all tax laws considered by the House and was a major influence on budget bills as Ways and Means chairman.
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden
item.category: House Committee on Ways and Means

media:content.media:credit: Scott Robinson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bill Archer in 1998. As chairman of the House Ways and Means Committee, he played a key role in writing legislation on taxes and spending. “We must stop measuring compassion by the amount of money the government spends,” he said in 1995.

item.title: Andy Baraghani’s Chicken Salad Is Full of Crunch
item.description: And, since it swaps cashew butter for the mayo, it can also stand the summer heat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Weinstein

media:content.media:credit: Linda Xiao for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.

item.title: The New, Unsustainable Art World
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay by a former global art director about the struggles that galleries face.

media:content.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
